files.00_Overview.md: Add Learning Objectives section (already has some LOs in list format but needs formal 3W structure). Add Key Takeaways section. Strengthen the 'Why' for each phenomenon section. Consider adding Prerequisites section (e.g., MODULE 04 multiphase fundamentals). Tutorial section lacks step-by-step What/How structure.
files.01_Phase_Change_Modeling.md: Missing Learning Objectives section entirely. Missing Key Takeaways section. 'What' is covered but 'Why' each model type matters needs strengthening. No Prerequisites stated. Tutorial/practice examples are absent. Needs comparison table: When to use Lee vs Hertz-Knudsen vs thermalPhaseChange with decision criteria.
files.02_Cavitation_Modeling.md: Missing Learning Objectives section. Missing Key Takeaways section. Strong on 'What' and 'How', but 'Why' cavitation modeling matters for engineering design could be more prominent. No Prerequisites. Good comparison tables exist - maintain these. Add a practical workflow section: Step-by-step cavitation case setup.
files.03_Population_Balance_Modeling.md: Missing Learning Objectives section. Missing Key Takeaways section. 'What' and 'How' are well-covered, but 'Why PBM matters' for industrial applications needs more emphasis. No Prerequisites. Good mathematical formulation. Consider adding a decision flowchart: When to use PBM vs constant diameter assumption.

global_strategy: Apply 3W Framework consistently across all files: (1) WHAT - clear definitions and terminology, (2) WHY - engineering significance and decision criteria for each model choice, (3) HOW - implementation details with OpenFOAM-specific code examples. Add standard sections to every file: Learning Objectives, Prerequisites, Key Takeaways, Practice Exercises. Eliminate redundancy: Cavitation setup appears in both 00_Overview and 02_Cavitation_Modeling - consolidate detailed implementation into 02, keep only high-level concepts in 00. Same for Phase Change content. Ensure progression: Overview should motivate and preview, not duplicate detailed content from specialized files.
